revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ar:أبي نو سيمي]][[bg:Абе-но Сеймей]][[ca:Abe no Seimei]][[cs:Abe no Seimei]][[en:Abe no Seimei]][[es:Abe no Seimei]][[fa:آبه نو سیمی]][[fr:Abe no Seimei]][[hu:Abe no Szeimei]][[id:Abe no Seimei]][[it:Abe no Seimei]][[ja:安倍晴明]][[ko:아베노 세이메이]][[pt:Abe no Seimei]][[ru:Абэ-но Сэймэй]][[sh:Abe no Seimei]][[tl:Abe no Seimei]][[tr:Abe no Seimei]][[vi:Abe no Seimei]][[wuu:安倍晴明]][[zh-min-nan:Abe no Seimei]][[zh:安倍晴明]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Abe Seimei.jpg|birth_date={{birth date|0921|2|26}}|death_date={{death date|1005|10|31}}; {{death date|1005|11|6}}|birth_place={{ill|Abeno-ku|qid=Q489009|1=Abeno-ku, Osaka}}|death_place={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17|1=Japan}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17|1=Japan}}|occupation={{ill|astrologer|qid=Q155647|1=Astrologer}}; {{ill|astronomer|qid=Q11063|1=Astronomer}}; {{ill|onmyōji|qid=Q2274163|1=Onmyōji}}|title={{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|qid=Q11071127|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}; {{ill|tenmon hakase|qid=Q11442648|1=Tenmon Hakase}}|children={{ill|Yoshihira Abe|qid=Q11450302|1=Abe no Yoshihira}}; {{ill|Yoshimasa Abe|qid=Q11450303|1=Yoshimasa}}|family={{ill|Abe clan|qid=Q11657247|1=Abe clan}}|notable_works={{ill|Senji Ryakketsu|qid=Q7450796|1=Senji Ryakketsu}}|religion={{ill|Onmyōdō|qid=Q1340540|1=Onmyōdō}}; {{ill|Shinto|qid=Q812767|1=Shinto}}|native_name=安倍 晴明|resting_place={{ill|Togetsu-kyō|qid=Q11561923|1=Togetsu-kyō}}}}
{{short description|Japanese mystic|qq=}} {{family name hatnote|Abe|lang=Japanese|qq=}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|安倍晴明|date=September 2016|qq=}} {{Infobox religious biography | background = #8B0000 | name = Abe no Seimei | image = Abe Seimei.jpg | caption = '''Abe no Seimei''' as drawn by {{ill|Kikuchi Yōsai|en|Kikuchi Yōsai|qid=Q700019|lt=Kikuchi Yōsai|1=Kikuchi Yōsai}} (菊池容斎), a popular painter in Japan | religion = ''[[Onmyōdō]]'' | alias = | location = Japan | Title = '''Onmyōji''' | Period = | Predecessor = | Successor = | ordination = | post = '''Onmyōji''' – adviser to the Emperor on the spiritually correct way to deal with issues. | birth_date = February 21, 921 A.D. | birth_place = – Abemonju-in Temple in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|Sakurai, Nara|qid=Q127026|lt=Sakurai, Nara|1=Sakurai, Nara}}, Japan<ref name="ShrineSite">{{cite web |year=2007 |url = http://www.taleofgenji.org/seimei.html|title = Seimei Shrine|publisher =The Tale of Genji| access-date = 2007-08-29 | archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20070821224638/http://www.taleofgenji.org/seimei.html| archive-date= 21 August 2007 | url-status= live|qq=}}</ref> | death_date = October 31, 1005 (aged 84) | death_place = {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} }} {{nihongo|'''Abe no Seimei'''|安倍 晴明||February 21, 921 AD – October 31, 1005|qq=}} was an ''[[onmyōji]]'', a leading specialist of ''[[Onmyōdō]]'' during the middleof the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} in Japan.<ref name="Miller33">Miller, Laura. "Extreme Makeover for a Heian-era Wizard". ''Mechademia 3: Limits of the Human''. Minneapolis: University of Minnesota Press, 2008. 33.</ref> In addition to his prominence in [[:en:Japanese history|history]], he is a legendary figure in [[Japanese folklore]]. He has been portrayed in several stories and films. Seimei worked as an ''onmyōji'' for emperors and the Heian government, advising on the spiritually correct way to deal with issues. He prayed for the well-being of emperors and the government and advised on various issues. He was also an {{ill|Astrology|en|Astrology|qid=Q34362|lt=astrologer|1=Astrology}} and predicted astrological events. He enjoyed an extremely long life, free from any major illness, contributing to the popular belief that he had [[magic (paranormal)|mystical]] powers. The [[Seimei Shrine]], located in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}, is dedicated to him. The Abeno {{ill|Ōsaka Abenobashi Station|en|Ōsaka Abenobashi Station|qid=Q6801405|lt=train station|1=Ōsaka Abenobashi Station}} and {{ill|Abeno-ku, Osaka|en|Abeno-ku, Osaka|qid=Q489009|lt=district|1=Abeno-ku, Osaka}} in Osaka are named after him, as it is one of the locations where legends place his birth. ==Life and legends== {{jmyth infobox|qq=}} Seimei's life is well recorded, and there is little to question about his existence. Immediately after his death, however, legends arose much like those surrounding [[:en:Merlin (wizard)|Merlin]]. Many legends of Seimei were originally written in the ''{{ill|Konjaku Monogatarishū|en|Konjaku Monogatarishū|qid=Q1507085|lt=Konjaku Monogatarishū|1=Konjaku Monogatarishū}}'', and by the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, many stories in circulation focused on his heroic acts. His ancestry was not very clear. His ancestor might have been Abe no Masuki (安倍 益材), a ''Daizen-no-daibu'' (大膳大夫 "Master of the Palace Table"),<ref name="Keizu">「安倍氏系図」（『続群書類従』巻第170 所収）、『[[:ja:尊卑分脈|尊卑分脈]]』、『諸家知譜拙記』</ref> or Abe no Shunzai (安倍 春材), a ''{{ill|Kokushi (official)|en|Kokushi (official)|qid=Q1351234|lt=Kokushi|1=Kokushi (official)}}'' of {{ill|Awaji Province|en|Awaji Province|qid=Q1053717|lt=Awaji|1=Awaji Province}}.<ref name="Suzuki">鈴木真年『百家系図稿』巻5,安倍（宝賀寿男『古代氏族系譜集成』古代氏族研究会、1986th year による）</ref> Another candidate is Abe no Miushi ([[:ja:阿倍御主人|阿倍 御主人]]), who appeared as an ''Udaijin'' (右大臣 "{{ill|Minister of the Right|en|Minister of the Right|qid=Q1351174|lt=Minister of the Right|1=Minister of the Right}}") in ''{{ill|The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter|en|The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter|qid=Q923593|lt=The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter|1=The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter}}''.<ref name="Keizu" /><ref name="Suzuki" /> Seimei might also have been a descendant of {{ill|Abe no Nakamaro|en|Abe no Nakamaro|qid=Q479788|lt=Abe no Nakamaro|1=Abe no Nakamaro}} as Abe-no-Sukune-no-Seimei (安倍宿禰晴明). However, some other sources recorded his name as Abe-no-Asomi-no-Seimei (安倍朝臣晴明), which refers to the Abe-no-Asomi descending from Abe no Miushi. The name [[Sukune]] (宿禰), through the Abe clan, was taken from Naniwa no Imiki (難波忌寸, later Naniwa no Sukune) of the Naniwa clan (難波氏), also known as the Naniwa no Kishi (難波吉士), which Naniwa no Mitsuna ([[:ja:難波三綱|難波 三綱]], {{fl.|672|qq=}}) established.<ref name="Edo">[[:ja:江戸時代|江戸時代]]に成立した説話「安倍仲丸転生記」（著者不詳）および[[:ja:講談|講談本]]「神道講釈安倍晴明」（[[:ja:旭堂南陵|旭堂南陵]]の口演を速記したもの）。</ref> According to Anderson,<ref>Anderson, William. ''Descriptive and Historical Catalogue of a Collection of Japanese and Chinese Paintings in the British Museum.'' London: Longman's & Co., 1886. 391.</ref> Abe no Seimei was a descendant of the poet {{ill|Abe no Nakamaro|en|Abe no Nakamaro|qid=Q479788|lt=Abe no Nakamaro|1=Abe no Nakamaro}} and a disciple of Kamo no Tadayuki ([[:ja:賀茂忠行|賀茂忠行]]) and {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|en|Kamo no Yasunori|qid=Q6358935|lt=Kamo no Yasunori|1=Kamo no Yasunori}}, 10th-century diviners of the Heian court. He became Kamo no Yasunori's successor in astrology and divination, while Yasunori's son took on the lesser responsibility of devising the calendar.<ref>Mikami, Yoshio. "The Development of Mathematics in China and Japan." ''Abhandlungen zur Geschichte der mathematischen Wissenschaften mit Einschluss ihrer Anwendungen.'' Volume XXX. 1913. 179.</ref><ref>Goff, Janet. ''Conjuring Kuzunoha from the World of Abe no Seimei''. ''A Kabuki Reader: History and Performance'', ed. Samuel L. Leiter. New York: M. E. Sharpe, 2001. 271. ({{ISBN|0-7656-0704-2|qq=}})</ref> Seimei's duties included analyzing strange events, conducting exorcisms, warding against evil spirits, and performing various rites of {{ill|Geomancy|en|Geomancy|qid=Q815750|lt=Geomancy|1=Geomancy}}. He was said to be incredibly skilled in divining the sex of fetuses and finding lost objects.<ref name="Miller33"/> According to the ''Konjaku Monogatarishu'', he correctly predicted the abdication of {{ill|Emperor Kazan|en|Emperor Kazan|qid=Q361000|lt=Emperor Kazan|1=Emperor Kazan}} based on his observation of celestial phenomena. Seimei's reputation grew sufficiently that, from the late 10th century, the ''Onmyōryō'', the government ministry of ''Onmyōdō'', was controlled by the Abe clan. The Kamo clan likewise became the hereditary keepers of the calendar.<ref>Itō, Satoshi. ''Shinto — a Short History.'' New York: RourledgeCurzon, 2003. 98. ({{ISBN|0-415-31179-9|qq=}})</ref> The mystical symbol of the central five-pointed star referred to in the West as a {{ill|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|en|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|qid=Q218652|lt=pentagram|1=Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)}}, is known in Japan as the ''Seiman'', the ''Seal of Abe no Seimei'', or the ''Seimei Kikyō'' (晴明紋 / 晴明桔梗); this pentacle was originally the personal seal of Abe-no-Seimei, later becoming the symbol for the ''[[Yin-Yang Bureau|Onmyōryō]]'' (the government ministry department for the practice of Onmyōdō, or the Bureau of Taoist Geomancy) and for Onmyōdō itself, given its association with the Chinese Five Elements.<ref>Miller. ''Extreme Makeover.'' 44</ref> According to legend, Abe no Seimei was {{ill|liminal being|en|liminal being|qid=Q6549417|lt=not entirely human|1=Liminal being}}. His father, Abe no Yasuna (安倍 保名), was human. Still, his mother, {{ill|Kuzunoha|en|Kuzunoha|qid=Q1651785|lt=Kuzunoha|1=Kuzunoha}}, was a ''{{ill|Kitsune|en|Kitsune|qid=Q692111|lt=Kitsune|1=Kitsune}}'' (a "fox spirit").<ref>Goff. ''Conjuring Kuzunoha.'' 269–270.</ref> At a very early age, no later than five, he was allegedly able to command weak ''{{ill|Oni|en|Oni|qid=Q730052|lt=Oni|1=Oni}}'' to do his bidding. His mother entrusted Seimei to Kamo no Tadayuki to live a good human life and not become evil himself. The Heian period, mainly when Seimei lived, was a time of peace. Many of his legends revolve around a series of magical battles with a rival, {{ill|Ashiya Dōman|en|Ashiya Dōman|qid=Q11641735|lt=Ashiya Dōman|1=Ashiya Dōman}} (蘆屋道満), who often tried to embarrass Seimei so that he could usurp his position. One noted story involved Dōman and the young Seimei in a {{ill|Divination|en|Divination|qid=Q1043197|lt=Divination|1=Divination}} duel to reveal the contents of a particular box. Dōman had another person put fifteen {{ill|Mandarin orange|en|Mandarin orange|qid=Q125337|lt=Mandarin orange|1=Mandarin orange}}s into the box and "divined" that there were fifteen oranges in it. Seimei saw through the ruse, metamorphosed the oranges into rats, and stated that fifteen rats were in the box. When the rats were revealed, Dōman was shocked and defeated. Seimei is involved in numerous other tales as well. He appears as a minor character in the ''[[:en:Heike Monogatari|Heike Monogatari]]'' and is said to be responsible for divining the location of the {{ill|Shuten-dōji|en|Shuten-dōji|qid=Q986527|lt=Shuten-dōji|1=Shuten-dōji}}, a powerful [[:en:Oni (folklore)|oni]] purportedly slain by {{ill|Minamoto no Yorimitsu|en|Minamoto no Yorimitsu|qid=Q590523|lt=Minamoto no Yorimitsu|1=Minamoto no Yorimitsu}}.<ref>Tanaka, Stefan. ''New Times in Modern Japan.'' New Jersey: Princeton University Press, 2004. 57–58. ({{ISBN|0-691-11774-8|qq=}})</ref> He is sometimes said to be the onmyōji who discovered [[Tamamo-no-Mae]]'s true nature, although the time of the Tamamo-no-Mae story does not coincide with Seimei's lifetime; other sources credit the act to a descendant, [[Abe no Yasuchika]].<ref>Gilbertson, E. "Japanese Archery and Archers". ''Transactions and Proceedings of the Japan Society of London''. Volume 4. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner and Co. Ltd., 1900. 118.</ref><ref>Schwarz, Karl M. ''Netsuke Subjects''. Vienna: Novographics, 1992. 72.</ref><ref>Kusano, Eisaburō. ''Stories Behind Noh and Kabuki Plays''. Tokyo: Tokyo News Service, 1962. 80.</ref> <gallery> File:Seimei-jinja torii.jpg|[[Torii]] of the Seimei {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}} File:Japanese_Crest_Abe_no_Seimei_Hann.svg|Seimei's pentagram [[mon (emblem)|mon]] represents the {{ill|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|en|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|qid=Q218652|lt=Wu Xing|1=Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)}}. File:Seeman douman.png|{{ill|Seiman Dōman|ja|セーマンドーマン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiman Dōman|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiman Dōman|qq=}} amulet, consisting of Seimei's pentagram and Dōman's lattice </gallery> ==Legacy== [[File:2018 Winter Olympics - Yuzuru Hanyu FS (1).jpg|thumb|upright|{{ill|Yuzuru Hanyu|en|Yuzuru Hanyu|qid=Q597023|lt=Yuzuru Hanyu|1=Yuzuru Hanyu}} in his free skate program ''[[Yuzuru Hanyu Olympic seasons#Free skate program: Seimei|Seimei]]'' at the {{ill|Figure skating at the 2018 Winter Olympics – Men's singles|en|Figure skating at the 2018 Winter Olympics – Men's singles|qid=Q47295100|lt=2018 Winter Olympics|1=Figure skating at the 2018 Winter Olympics – Men's singles}}]] After Seimei's death, {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|en|Emperor Ichijō|qid=Q449540|lt=Emperor Ichijo|1=Emperor Ichijō}} made a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in his honor, the Seimei Jinja was erected at the location of his home in 1007 AD.<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal|last=Miller|first=Laura|date=2011|title=A wizard shrine charms girls and women|url=https://popanth.com/article/a-wizard-shrine-charms-girls-and-women|journal=Japan Notes|pages=3|via=PopAnth|access-date=2019-02-20|archive-date=2021-10-24|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211024155350/https://popanth.com/article/a-wizard-shrine-charms-girls-and-women|url-status=dead|qq=}}</ref> The original shrine was destroyed in war during the fifteenth century, but it was rebuilt in the same location and still stands today.<ref name="ShrineSite"/><ref>Dougill, John. ''Kyoto: a Cultural History.'' New York: Oxford University Press, 2005. 19.</ref> The Seimei Jinja located in Kyoto attracts fans of Seimei's interpretations in popular culture. In 2005, the shrine was renovated and decorated with pentagrams. The shrine sells amulets with pentagrams and the [[:en:Chinese bellflower|Chinese bellflower]] adorning them, associated with Seimei. The shrine is now popular with fans of Seimei-inspired media, such as Okano Reiko's manga.<ref name=":0" /> The {{ill|Asteroid|en|Asteroid|qid=Q3863|lt=Asteroid|1=Asteroid}} {{ill|5541 Seimei|en|5541 Seimei|qid=Q261245|lt=5541 Seimei|1=5541 Seimei}}, discovered in 1976, is named for him.<ref>Schmadel, Lutz D. ''Dictionary of Minor Planet Names.'' Berlin: Springer-Verlag, 1992. 472. ({{ISBN|3-540-00238-3|qq=}})</ref> Japanese figure skater and two-time Olympic champion {{ill|Yuzuru Hanyu|en|Yuzuru Hanyu|qid=Q597023|lt=Yuzuru Hanyu|1=Yuzuru Hanyu}} portrayed Abe no Seimei in his Olympic {{ill|Free skating|en|Free skating|qid=Q4380244|lt=free skate|1=Free skating}} program ''[[:en:Seimei (figure skating program)|Seimei]]'', using the soundtrack of the movies ''[[:en:Onmyōji (film)|Onmyōji]]'' and ''[[:en:The Yin Yang Master 2|Onmyōji II]]''. With the program, Hanyu became the first skater across all disciplines to score above 200 points in the free skate and set back-to-back [[List of career achievements by Yuzuru Hanyu#Historical world record scores|world records]] in the 2015–16 season.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://english.kyodonews.net/news/2017/08/85d4fe54983a-figure-skating-hanyu-to-skate-to-onmyoji-during-olympic-season.html|title=Figure skating: Hanyu to skate to "Onmyoji" during Olympic season|work={{ill|Kyodo News|en|Kyodo News|qid=Q779730|lt=Kyodo News|1=Kyodo News}}|location={{ill|Minato, Tokyo|en|Minato, Tokyo|qid=Q190088|lt=Minato, Tokyo|1=Minato, Tokyo}}|date=August 9, 2017|archive-date=January 4, 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190104040004/https://english.kyodonews.net/news/2017/08/85d4fe54983a-figure-skating-hanyu-to-skate-to-onmyoji-during-olympic-season.html|url-status=live |qq=}}</ref> This program also contributed to the win of his [[Yuzuru Hanyu Olympic seasons#2018 Winter Olympics|second Olympic title]] in 2018 and the completion of the first career [[Grand Slam (figure skating)#Super Slam|Super Slam]] in the men's singles discipline in 2020.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://nonno.hpplus.jp/article/65252/02/|title=羽生結弦 – フィギュアスケート史に燦然と輝く栄光の軌跡 |trans-title=Yuzuru Hanyu – The track of glory shining brightly in the history of figure skating|website=nonno.hpplus.jp|publisher={{ill|Shueisha|en|Shueisha|qid=Q844822|lt=Shueisha|1=Shueisha}}|location={{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}|language=ja|date=October 7, 2021|archive-date=October 8, 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211008111631/https://nonno.hpplus.jp/article/65252/02/|url-status=live |qq=}}</ref> Abe no Seimei's origin story would be retold on the {{ill|Anime|en|Anime|qid=Q1107|lt=Japanese Animation|1=Anime}} television show ''{{ill|Folktales from Japan|en|Folktales from Japan|qid=Q3754872|lt=Folktales from Japan|1=Folktales from Japan}}'' in episode 83. His story would appear in the third segment of the episode, telling the tale of the meeting of parents and early life known as Doujimaru. It would show how his mother commanded him to become a fortune teller at the capital to help others. Abe no seimei was termed the "[[:en:Merlin the Magician|Merlin the Magician]] of Japan" by some scholars due to his legends and portrayals in several stories, folklore, and films – also due to the legends of Merlin being a {{ill|cambion|en|cambion|qid=Q2934676|lt=cambion|1=Cambion}} of a demon and a human much like the {{ill|Kuzunoha|en|Kuzunoha|qid=Q1651785|lt=Kuzunoha|1=Kuzunoha}} legend. ===''Senji Ryakketsu''=== Abe no Seimei is credited with the ''[[Senji Ryakketsu]]'', an ''Onmyōdō'' primer. ==In fiction== {{Cleanup rewrite|date=January 2018|2=section|qq=}} His name appears in many works of fiction, often as a helpful, wise man and rarely as an enemy, such as ''{{ill|Twin Star Exorcists|en|Twin Star Exorcists|qid=Q17228961|lt=Twin Star Exorcists|1=Twin Star Exorcists}}''. There are exceptions, such as ''{{ill|Nura: Rise of the Yokai Clan|en|Nura: Rise of the Yokai Clan|qid=Q1194085|lt=Nura: Rise of the Yokai Clan|1=Nura: Rise of the Yokai Clan}}'', where Seimei was also a great ayakashi and the Lord of Darkness. Since 1989, Abe no Seimei has been depicted as a {{ill|Bishōnen|en|Bishōnen|qid=Q866369|lt=Bishōnen|1=Bishōnen}}.<ref>{{cite journal |last=Miller |first=Laura |title=Extreme Makeover for a Heian-Era Wizard |url=https://luc.academia.edu/documents/0009/9900/Extreme_Makeover_for_a_Heian-Era_Wizard.pdf |url-status=dead |journal=[[Mechademia]] |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100619234938/http://luc.academia.edu/documents/0009/9900/Extreme_Makeover_for_a_Heian-Era_Wizard.pdf |archive-date=2010-06-19|qq=}}</ref> === Novels === The first modern fictional work credited with bringing back widespread interest to ''Onmyōdō'' mysticism in Japan is the 1985 {{ill|Historical fantasy|en|Historical fantasy|qid=Q603291|lt=Historical fantasy|1=Historical fantasy}} novel ''{{ill|Teito Monogatari|en|Teito Monogatari|qid=Q2421857|lt=Teito Monogatari|1=Teito Monogatari}}'' by {{ill|Hiroshi Aramata|en|Hiroshi Aramata|qid=Q11618411|lt=Hiroshi Aramata|1=Hiroshi Aramata}}.<ref>Kazuhiko, Komatsu. "Seimei jinja" 28–61</ref> In the novel's story, two of the primary characters, {{ill|Yasumasa Hirai|en|Yasumasa Hirai|qid=Q8050032|lt=Yasumasa Hirai|1=Yasumasa Hirai}} and {{ill|Yasunori Katō|en|Yasunori Katō|qid=Q1983782|lt=Yasunori Katō|1=Yasunori Katō}}, are descendants of Seimei and have inherited all his knowledge. Yasumasa Hirai is a notable example because his appearance is modeled off classic depictions of Seimei, and many of his actions are based on those of Seimei's from stories in the ''{{ill|Uji Shūi Monogatari|en|Uji Shūi Monogatari|qid=Q2446713|lt=Uji Shūi Monogatari|1=Uji Shūi Monogatari}}''.<ref>Reider, Noriko T. ''Japanese Demon Lore: Oni from Ancient Times to the Present'' Utah State University Press, 2010. 113. ({{ISBN|0874217938|qq=}})</ref> {{ill|Yasunori Katō|en|Yasunori Katō|qid=Q1983782|lt=Yasunori Katō|1=Yasunori Katō}}'s first name "Yasunori" is derived from the name of Seimei's {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|en|Kamo no Yasunori|qid=Q6358935|lt=legendary teacher|1=Kamo no Yasunori}} and he proudly wears Seimei's symbol, the Seiman (five-pointed star), on his gloves and handkerchief. Unlike Hirai, though, Katō's burning hatred for the Japanese Empire has metamorphosed him into an {{ill|Oni|en|Oni|qid=Q730052|lt=Oni|1=Oni}}. With one in defense of the Empire and one against it, the two men naturally become enemies. The rest of the novel chronicles the battle between their two factions. In 1988, {{ill|Baku Yumemakura|en|Baku Yumemakura|qid=Q2880354|lt=Baku Yumemakura|1=Baku Yumemakura}} started a novel series named ''[[:en:Onmyōji (novel series)|Onmyōji]]'' with Seimei portrayed as a handsome young man who lived in a Heian-period world populated by mysterious beings. Reiko Okano turned this into a manga and became popular with teenage girls. In 2002, an NHK television series was made based on the novels.<ref name="jdorama1">{{cite web |year=2007 |url = http://jdorama.com/drama.611.htm|title = Onmyouji 陰陽師| access-date = 2007-08-29 | archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20071013024428/http://jdorama.com/drama.611.htm| archive-date= 13 October 2007 | url-status= live|qq=}}</ref> A version of Abe has also been rendered by acclaimed Taiwanese manga artist Ethan, who has stated that he is a massive fan of the novel.<ref name="Onion-Club">{{cite web| year= 2007| url= http://anarchy-manga.com/Onions/onion-club-manga-emoticons-about.php?page=6| title= The Onion Club, E's Past Works| access-date= 2007-11-09| archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20071119045651/http://anarchy-manga.com/Onions/onion-club-manga-emoticons-about.php?page=6| archive-date= 19 November 2007| url-status= dead| df= dmy-all|qq=}}</ref> The novels were adapted into an anime by the studio Marvy Jack for Netflix in November 2023. === Films === The film ''[[:en:Onmyōji (film)|Onmyōji]]'', starring {{ill|Mansai Nomura|en|Mansai Nomura|qid=Q910611|lt=Mansai Nomura|1=Mansai Nomura}} as Seimei, was released in {{ill|2001 in film|en|2001 in film|qid=Q753352|lt=2001|1=2001 in film}} (2004 in the {{ill|United States|en|United States|qid=Q30|lt=U.S.|1=United States}}) by Pioneer (now [[:en:Geneon|Geneon]]). As with any other work featuring both Seimei and {{ill|Minamoto no Hiromasa|en|Minamoto no Hiromasa|qid=Q3276201|lt=Minamoto no Hiromasa|1=Minamoto no Hiromasa}}, the film was based on Yumemakura's novels. Despite Yumemakura being involved, the manga adaptation and the movie adaptation are quite different in style and plot. A sequel, ''[[:en:Onmyōji 2|Onmyōji 2]]'', was made in 2003.{{sfn|Galbraith IV|2008|p=420|qq=}} In 2020, a Chinese film, ''{{ill|The Yin-Yang Master: Dream of Eternity|en|The Yin-Yang Master: Dream of Eternity|qid=Q104489028|lt=The Yin-Yang Master: Dream of Eternity|1=The Yin-Yang Master: Dream of Eternity}}'', was produced based on an adaption from ''Onmyōji'' written by {{ill|Baku Yumemakura|en|Baku Yumemakura|qid=Q2880354|lt=Baku Yumemakura|1=Baku Yumemakura}}.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Kundu |first1=Tamal |date=23 March 2021 |title=Yin-Yang Master 2 Release Date: Will There be a Dream of Eternity Sequel? |work=The Cinemaholic |url=https://thecinemaholic.com/will-there-be-a-sequel-to-yin-yang-master-dream-of-eternity/ |access-date=2 April 2021 |archive-date=22 May 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230522074017/https://thecinemaholic.com/will-there-be-a-sequel-to-yin-yang-master-dream-of-eternity/ |url-status=dead |qq=}}</ref> Seimei was portrayed by {{ill|Mark Chao|en|Mark Chao|qid=Q710955|lt=Mark Chao|1=Mark Chao}}. In the following year, another Chinese film, ''{{ill|The Yinyang Master|en|The Yinyang Master|qid=Q105413958|lt=The Yinyang Master|1=The Yinyang Master}}'' was released. The film is an adaption of a {{ill|NetEase|en|NetEase|qid=Q196259|lt=NetEase|1=NetEase}} game ''Onmyōji'' (which in turn is based on the novel series ''Onmyōji'' by author Baku Yumemakura).<ref name="game2">{{cite web |last=Rui |first=Zhang |date=2021-02-09 |title='The Yinyang Master' brings mobile game to the big screen |url=http://www.china.org.cn/arts/2021-02/09/content_77204857.htm |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210210021658/http://www.china.org.cn/arts/2021-02/09/content_77204857.htm |archive-date=2021-02-10 |access-date=2021-02-18 |publisher=[[China Internet Information Center|qq=}}</ref><ref name="hr0209">{{cite web |last=Brzeski |first=Patrick |date=2021-02-09 |title=Netflix Acquires Chinese Fantasy Film 'The Yin Yang Master' |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/news/netflix-acquires-chinese-film-the-yin-yang-master |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210209131449/https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/news/netflix-acquires-chinese-film-the-yin-yang-master |archive-date=2021-02-09 |access-date=2021-02-09 |publisher=[[Hollywood Reporter|qq=}}</ref> Seimei was portrayed by {{ill|Chen Kun|en|Chen Kun|qid=Q706092|lt=Chen Kun|1=Chen Kun}}.<ref name="game">{{cite web |last=Rui |first=Zhang |date=2021-02-09 |title='The Yinyang Master' brings mobile game to the big screen |url=http://www.china.org.cn/arts/2021-02/09/content_77204857.htm |access-date=2021-02-18 |publisher=[[China Internet Information Center|qq=}}</ref> === Television === To capitalize on the success of the ''Onmyōji'' films, {{ill|Fuji Television|en|Fuji Television|qid=Q744800|lt=Fuji Television|1=Fuji Television}} produced a miniseries in 2004, called ''Onmyoji: Abe no Seimei''.<ref name="jdorama2">{{cite web |year=2007 |title=Onmyouji 陰陽師 |url=http://jdorama.com/drama.693.htm |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071012231035/http://jdorama.com/drama.693.htm |archive-date=12 October 2007 |access-date=2007-08-29|qq=}}</ref> This series has no ties to cinematic releases. === Manga === Hao Asakura's character from {{ill|Hiroyuki Takei|en|Hiroyuki Takei|qid=Q534001|lt=Hiroyuki Takei|1=Hiroyuki Takei}}'s ''{{ill|Shaman King|en|Shaman King|qid=Q219610|lt=Shaman King|1=Shaman King}}'' is directly based on Seimei. Hao is the author of a magical book called Chō-Senjiryakketsu, clearly inspired by Seimei's ''[[Senji Ryakketsu]]''. They also share facts about their lives, such as their mother being called a demon fox and their ability to create ''{{ill|Oni|en|Oni|qid=Q730052|lt=Oni|1=Oni}}'' since they were young. Seimei had been shown in a manga called ''[[:en:Nurarihyon no Mago|Nurarihyon no Mago]]'' by {{ill|Hiroshi Shiibashi|en|Hiroshi Shiibashi|qid=Q2532959|lt=Hiroshi Shiibashi|1=Hiroshi Shiibashi}}, as a {{ill|Nue|en|Nue|qid=Q1165886|lt=Nue|1=Nue}}, dark lord of the Ayakashi, born from an evil fox. ''Nurarihyon no Mago'' was adapted into an anime series starting in July 2010. Abe no Seimei also appears in the manga Igyoujin Oniwakamaru as an evil spirit who plans to revive himself to begin his second life and rule over humans and yokai. He also appeared in {{ill|Kouta Hirano|en|Kouta Hirano|qid=Q445903|lt=Kouta Hirano|1=Kouta Hirano}}'s ''{{ill|Drifters (manga)|en|Drifters (manga)|qid=Q2702432|lt=Drifters|1=Drifters (manga)}}'' manga series leading a group of magicians called the Octobrist Organization. Appearing as a young man, he has been pulled into an alternate world where various historical figures are summoned, and magicians need their skills and techniques to save their world from destruction. It is mentioned in English dubbed anime of ''Drifters'' that Abe no Haruakira was another way to read the kanji of his name. In the manga series ''{{ill|Twin Star Exorcists|en|Twin Star Exorcists|qid=Q17228961|lt=Twin Star Exorcists|1=Twin Star Exorcists}}'', Seimei was, in fact, a female exorcist no older than thirteen. She was the founder of yang-style exorcism who did away with the King of Kegare (Impurities), creating Magano as a powerful barrier and forcefield. Before shutting herself and her retainers inside, she turned to her disciple Doujimaru and granted him a some of her powers. This Doujimaru that modern times know as 'Abe no Seimei'. (The truth about Seimei being female is known only to a few characters in-universe). In contrast to the source material, however, the second half of its anime adaptation with its completely original story in comparison to the source has Seimei still be male. He serves as the final antagonist of the anime-only storyline, with his plans to use the Cataclysm King that is Rokuro to cleanse humanity by erasing humanity's free will. === Anime === Seimei can be seen in the anime ''{{ill|Magical Shopping Arcade Abenobashi|en|Magical Shopping Arcade Abenobashi|qid=Q2465820|lt=Magical Shopping Arcade Abenobashi|1=Magical Shopping Arcade Abenobashi}}'', released in 2004 in the U.S. by [[:en:ADV Films|ADV Films]]. The show's focus was on the Onmyoji practice of changing events to avoid an ill occurrence and the misadventures of two youths. Seimei also appears in the anime ''{{ill|Gintama|en|Gintama|qid=Q764135|lt=Gintama|1=Gintama}}'' as an onmyoji, and in the anime ''[[:en:Shonen Onmyouji|Shonen Onmyouji]]'' which is about his grandson. Seimei is a central character in the anime called ''{{ill|Otogi Zoshi (TV series)|en|Otogi Zoshi (TV series)|qid=Q337985|lt=Otogi Zoshi|1=Otogi Zoshi (TV series)}}''. Seimei appears in the anime ''{{ill|New Getter Robo|en|New Getter Robo|qid=Q3616151|lt=New Getter Robo|1=New Getter Robo}}'' as the leader of the villainous oni. The second season of the anime ''{{ill|Garo: The Animation|en|Garo: The Animation|qid=Q18044187|lt=Garo: The Animation|1=Garo: The Animation}}'' features a female Abe no Seimei as its protagonist. He also appears in the anime ''Fukigen no Mononokean'' with Ashiya as his companion rather than his rival. Seimei plays a role in the ''{{ill|Midnight Occult Civil Servants|en|Midnight Occult Civil Servants|qid=Q55391811|lt=Midnight Occult Civil Servants|1=Midnight Occult Civil Servants}}'' anime, about mild-mannered civil servants in Tokyo's Shinjuku ward who secretly manage a huge population of fairies and other magical creatures. === Games === The horror/survival {{ill|PlayStation 2|en|PlayStation 2|qid=Q10680|lt=PlayStation 2|1=PlayStation 2}} video game {{ill|Kuon|en|Kuon|qid=Q2655757|lt=Kuon|1=Kuon}} featured Seimei as a female exorcist who becomes a playable character near the end of the game. Abe no Seimei was made a playable character in the {{ill|PlayStation 4|en|PlayStation 4|qid=Q5014725|lt=PlayStation 4|1=PlayStation 4}}, {{ill|PlayStation Portable|en|PlayStation Portable|qid=Q170325|lt=PlayStation Portable|1=PlayStation Portable}}, {{ill|Xbox One|en|Xbox One|qid=Q13361286|lt=Xbox One|1=Xbox One}}, and {{ill|Wii U|en|Wii U|qid=Q56942|lt=Wii U|1=Wii U}} versions of ''{{ill|Warriors Orochi 3|en|Warriors Orochi 3|qid=Q1101241|lt=Warriors Orochi 3|1=Warriors Orochi 3}}''. Seimei appears in the video game ''{{ill|Toukiden: The Age of Demons|en|Toukiden: The Age of Demons|qid=Q7828851|lt=Toukiden: The Age of Demons|1=Toukiden: The Age of Demons}}'' as a mitama (a hero's soul from Japanese history). In Part 1.5 of the mobile game ''{{ill|Fate/Grand Order|en|Fate/Grand Order|qid=Q18339082|lt=Fate/Grand Order|1=Fate/Grand Order}}'', Seimei's name is claimed as an undercover pseudonym used by {{ill|Ashiya Dōman|en|Ashiya Dōman|qid=Q11641735|lt=Ashiya Dōman|1=Ashiya Dōman}} in the Shimōsa story chapter. Later, Seimei indirectly appears briefly in Part 2's Heian-kyō story chapter as an ally to the protagonist. ''[[Onmyōji|Onmyoji]]'', a mobile game, featured Abe no Seimei as the protagonist who summons various [[Shikigami]] to ward off demons alongside his onmyoji comrades. In ''{{ill|Dragalia Lost|en|Dragalia Lost|qid=Q56290965|lt=Dragalia Lost|1=Dragalia Lost}}'', Seimei plays a part as head of the Ox Clan, one of the twelve Hinomotoan clans serving Amaterasu, with his soul pact dragon [[Gozu Tennō]] and his {{ill|Kitsune|en|Kitsune|qid=Q692111|lt=Kitsune|1=Kitsune}} disciple {{ill|Kuzunoha|en|Kuzunoha|qid=Q1651785|lt=Kuzunoha|1=Kuzunoha}}. Seimei appears as an NPC in the video game ''{{ill|Nioh 2|en|Nioh 2|qid=Q67482292|lt=Nioh 2|1=Nioh 2}}''. Despite being from the Heian period, he strangely appears during the Sengoku era to train the players in the art of Onmyō magic. It is later revealed in the DLC "Darkness in the Capital" that this individual was an avatar created by the real Seimei, who had sacrificed himself alongside Minamoto no Yorimitsu to seal away Shuten Dōji and Tamamo-no-Mae within [[Byōdō-in]] during the Heian period. Seimei appears in the {{ill|PlayStation Vita|en|PlayStation Vita|qid=Q188808|lt=PlayStation Vita|1=PlayStation Vita}} game ''{{ill|Oreshika: Tainted Bloodlines|en|Oreshika: Tainted Bloodlines|qid=Q16267742|lt=Oreshika: Tainted Bloodlines|1=Oreshika: Tainted Bloodlines}}'' as the main villain of the game, a priest who places a curse on the main characters that shortens their lifespan to only two years. ==References== {{Reflist|qq=}} ===Cited works=== {{refbegin|qq=}} * {{cite book |last=Galbraith IV |first=Stuart |title=The Toho Studios Story: A History and Complete Filmography |publisher=[[Scarecrow Press]] |year=2008 |isbn=978-1461673743|qq=}} {{refend|qq=}} == External links == {{Portal|Japan|Mythology|Ancient Japan|Biography|qq=}} {{Commons Category|Abe no Seimei|qq=}}
[[en:Abe no Seimei]]
<!---Abe-shi no Keizu "Genealogy of the Abe clan"---><!---Suzuki Matoshi---><!--- また、一部の古文書では安倍朝臣晴明ではなく安倍宿禰晴明と記載されるものが散見されるため、「朝臣」を「宿禰」の上位に厳格に位置づける当時の慣習から考えて、安倍御主人からの安倍朝臣姓の家系ではなく、難波吉士族の難波忌寸（のち宿禰）の末裔ではないかとする説もある。 However, because Abe no Sukune Seimei, instead of Abe no Asomi Seimei, appears often in a group of ancient documents, and based on the practice at that time of strictly placing "Asomi" above "Sukune," there is a view that considers him as a descendant of Naniwa no Imiki (later Sukune) of the Naniwa no Kishi clan instead of a family that bears the Abe no Asomi family name that originates from Abe no Miushi.---><!--Abe no Seimei-->
<!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[ar:أبي نو سيمي]] [[bg:Абе-но Сеймей]] [[ca:Abe no Seimei]] [[cs:Abe no Seimei]] [[es:Abe no Seimei]] [[fa:آبه نو سیمی]] [[fr:Abe no Seimei]] [[hu:Abe no Szeimei]] [[id:Abe no Seimei]] [[it:Abe no Seimei]] [[ja:安倍晴明]] [[ko:아베노 세이메이]] [[pt:Abe no Seimei]] [[ru:Абэ-но Сэймэй]] [[sh:Abe no Seimei]] [[tl:Abe no Seimei]] [[tr:Abe no Seimei]] [[vi:Abe no Seimei]] [[wuu:安倍晴明]] [[zh:安倍晴明]] [[zh-min-nan:Abe no Seimei]]
